notes: Review #11. Solid aesthetic improvement this cycle — the SVG character portraits are genuinely charming and the vibe-reactive theming is cohesive. However, a critical CSS bug silently kills the dust-particle effect entirely, error/done workers are invisible to the dashboard, and an ever-growing animation count with zero prefers-reduced-motion support is a real accessibility and battery-life liability. The getTeamVibe duplication survived from last review — starting to feel personal. Fun factor stays at 5; no amount of bugs can ruin Bloberto.

suggestions.title: Dust particles don't render — box-shadow percentages are invalid CSS
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 653-661: the .office-floor::before pseudo-element uses box-shadow with percentage x/y offsets (e.g. '12% 18% 0 1px rgba(...)'). CSS box-shadow offsets must be length values (px, rem, em) — percentages are not valid here. The spec rejects them silently, so the entire dust-particle effect is invisible. Replace with pixel values or switch to absolute-positioned pseudo-random <span> elements with CSS custom properties for coordinates. A canvas overlay is another clean option.

suggestions.title: Workers with 'done' or 'error' status are completely invisible on the dashboard
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 441: activeWorkers filters to only 'working' and 'idle'. Workers in 'done' or 'error' states never appear in the workers-grid, never contribute to StatsBar totals, and never render in the Office (since Office also receives activeWorkers). The Dashboard even has fully styled .stat-card.done and .worker-card.error CSS — but those states can never render. Add 'done' and 'error' to the activeWorkers filter (or rename it to visibleWorkers), and add a Done count to StatsBar. Error workers are especially critical — the vibe pill can say '🚨 On Fire' while the cause remains completely hidden.

suggestions.title: Zero prefers-reduced-motion support across 15+ concurrent animations
suggestions.description: At full load the app can run: manager-wobble, bob (×N working), wander-1/2/3/4 (×N idle), typing-dot ×3 (×N), plant-sway ×3, dust-drift, error-halo, vibe-crush/fire/in-flow, ghost-float, standby-pulse, pulse-live, shimmer, card-rise simultaneously. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block anywhere. This is a real issue for users with vestibular disorders and a battery drain on mobile. Add a single prefers-reduced-motion block that sets animation: none on all cosmetic animations, keeping only the error-halo (safety signal) and progress-bar transition.

suggestions.title: getTeamVibe still duplicated — survived from Review #10 unaddressed
suggestions.description: The function is defined in both App.jsx (returns {label, key} object) and Office.jsx (returns a plain string). The thresholds are identical so they don't diverge *yet*, but this is a maintenance trap. When someone tweaks the thresholds in one file, the vibe pill, stats bar, and office floor will disagree. Extract to src/utils/vibe.js, export the object-returning version, and import in both files. Office.jsx can destructure .key where it only needs the string.

suggestions.title: Character tooltips are hover-only — inaccessible on touch and keyboard
suggestions.description: The data-tooltip CSS trick (.char[data-tooltip]::after) only fires on :hover. On mobile touch, working characters' task descriptions and ghost characters' role labels are completely invisible. Keyboard users focusing past the office panel also get nothing. Since the office is role='img', interactive info really belongs in the Dashboard panel — but if you want tooltips in the Office, use a small React tooltip component triggered by both mouseenter and focus, with role='tooltip' and aria-describedby wiring. At minimum add a title attribute matching data-tooltip as a low-effort fallback.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS: hire-form, hq-badge, and stat-card.done rules reference nothing in JSX
suggestions.description: App.css contains full styling blocks for .hire-form-wrapper, .hire-form, .form-group, .hq-badge, and .stat-card.done — none of these classes are rendered anywhere in App.jsx or Office.jsx. The hire form was presumably removed at some point. Dead CSS adds confusion ('is this used somewhere I missed?'), inflates bundle size, and makes future refactors harder. Remove them or, if the hire form is coming back, at least put a TODO comment on the CSS block.

suggestions.title: Worker cards are keyboard-focusable but pressing Enter/Space does nothing
suggestions.description: WorkerCard has tabIndex={0} and a :focus-visible outline (good!) but no onKeyDown handler. A keyboard user tabbing through cards expects Enter or Space to do *something* — expand details, link to a profile, or at minimum trigger a visual acknowledgement. Either add a keydown handler that provides a meaningful action (e.g. announce the task via aria-live), or remove tabIndex and place focusable elements (like a 'View details' button) inside the card instead. Focusable elements that do nothing are worse than non-focusable ones.

suggestions.title: Character name labels in the Office view are illegibly small at default zoom
suggestions.description: Office.css: .char__name is 0.58rem (~9px). The 480px breakpoint hides them entirely. At 0.58rem on a 16px base that's ~9.3px — below the 11px minimum that most accessibility guidelines recommend even for decorative text. The names are a key wayfinding element in the office ('which blob is which?'). Bump to 0.7rem minimum. On 480px it's reasonable to hide them, but between 481-600px they should be visible and legible.

suggestions.title: getTeamVibe(activeWorkers) called 3x per render in App.jsx header with no memoization
suggestions.description: The App render calls getTeamVibe(activeWorkers) twice for the vibe-pill element (once for data-vibe, once for the label text) and StatsBar calls it a third time internally. Hoist to const vibe = useMemo(() => getTeamVibe(activeWorkers), [activeWorkers]) near the top of the App component and pass vibe as a prop to StatsBar. The function itself is cheap but the pattern scales poorly as more components reference vibe state.

suggestions.title: Idle wander paths clip through second-row desks
suggestions.description: Office.css wander-1 through wander-4 use top values as low as 73%. The second row of desks sits at top: 50% (DESKS array). Characters positioned at the desk have their avatar offset to top: 46% (desk.top - 4). The wander zones start at 73% which visually clears the desks, but on certain viewport widths where aspect-ratio: auto kicks in and the floor compresses vertically, idle characters can visually overlap the desk furniture. Set a hard floor for wander top values at 78%+ and test at 768px viewport width.

suggestions.title: Inline IIFE inside JSX for task tags is unnecessarily convoluted
suggestions.description: WorkerCard lines 196-208 use {(() => { const tags = getTaskTags(worker.task); return tags.length > 0 ? (...) : null })()} — an IIFE inside JSX. This is hard to read, harder to test, and adds no functional value over a simple extracted component. Replace with a <TaskTags task={worker.task} /> component that returns null when no tags match. This also makes the task-tag rendering independently testable.

suggestions.title: No worker status filter on the Dashboard workers grid
suggestions.description: The Activity Log has a filter bar (All / Hires / Completions / Errors) but the workers grid has no equivalent. Once error/done workers are visible (see suggestion #2), users will want to quickly filter by status. A simple pill-style filter strip above the workers-grid — matching the exact pattern already used for the activity filter — would complete the UX parity. Reuse the .activity-filter-bar CSS; the component pattern is already there.
